name: Claude Code Sandbox Prompt Test
description: Create a repo with deterministic setup, build a prompt-driven SvelteKit app in a local working copy, push it, then capture browser screenshots and video from a sandbox clone.

document.name: claude-code-sandbox-prompt-test
document.title: Claude Code Sandbox Prompt Test
document.summary: Deterministic repo setup, one prompt-driven Claude build step in a local working copy, deterministic git finalize/push, then minimal sandbox browser capture.

input.promptPlaceholder: Describe the SvelteKit app you want Claude to build in the repository.

model.label: Claude Model
model.description: Model used for the Claude implementation step.

with.prompt:
Set up a bare-ready Git repository for later sandbox work.

Use ONE Bash tool call only. Copy this script exactly and do not rename the owner, repo, or paths:

set -euo pipefail
OWNER='{{trigger.owner}}'
REPO='{{trigger.repo}}'
DESCRIPTION='Repository for prompt-driven Claude sandbox test'
API='http://gitea-http.gitea.svc.cluster.local:3000/api/v1'
CLONE_URL="http://giteaadmin:${GITEA_TOKEN}@gitea-http.gitea.svc.cluster.local:3000/${OWNER}/${REPO}.git"

if ! curl -sf "${API}/repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}" -H "Authorization: token ${GITEA_TOKEN}" >/dev/null; then
  curl -sS -X POST "${API}/user/repos" \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -H "Authorization: token ${GITEA_TOKEN}" \
    -d "{\"name\":\"${REPO}\",\"description\":\"${DESCRIPTION}\",\"private\":false,\"auto_init\":true}"
fi

rm -rf "/tmp/${REPO}"
git clone "${CLONE_URL}" "/tmp/${REPO}"
cd "/tmp/${REPO}"
git config user.email 'claude@agent.local'
git config user.name 'Claude Code Agent'
if ! git rev-parse HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf '# %s\n' "${REPO}" > README.md
  git add README.md
  git commit -m 'Initial commit'
  git push origin main
fi

git remote -v
git log --oneline -3 || true
echo 'SETUP COMPLETE'

agentConfig.name: claude-setup
agentConfig.instructions: You are setting up a new Git repository. Use curl for API calls and git for repo operations.

with.prompt:
Work only inside /tmp/{{trigger.repo}}. That directory is already a git clone of the target repository. Build the requested SvelteKit application from scratch in this repository based on the user prompt below.

Important execution rules:
- Use Bash, Read, Write, Glob, and Grep only.
- When you need to change a file, rewrite the whole file with Write instead of trying to patch it incrementally.
- Start by replacing the default SvelteKit starter page at src/routes/+page.svelte. The final app must not contain the text "Welcome to SvelteKit" anywhere in src/routes/+page.svelte.
- Build a standard npm-based SvelteKit app that runs with NODE_ENV=development npm install, npm run dev, npm run check, and npm run build.
- Follow standard SvelteKit validation practice: make sure the project has a check script that runs `svelte-kit sync && svelte-check --fail-on-warnings`, then run that check before the build.
- Use a current compatible SvelteKit toolchain. Do not invent arbitrary old version ranges. The project must include these devDependencies with compatible modern versions: @sveltejs/adapter-auto, @sveltejs/kit, @sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte, svelte, vite, and svelte-check.
- Include either jsconfig.json or tsconfig.json so svelte-check has a project config to use.
- The app must include these exact stable browser demo hooks in src/routes/+page.svelte:
  - data-demo="app-shell" on the main application container
  - data-demo="primary-action" on one visible interactive control
  - data-demo="demo-state" on one visible region that reflects the result of the interaction
- Clicking the primary action must visibly update the demo-state region.
- Produce valid Svelte markup and valid JavaScript. Do not leave invalid table structure, invalid nesting, compile warnings, or illegal JS syntax in the final app.
- Never emit JavaScript numeric literals with leading zeroes such as `095` or `045`; use normal decimal numbers like `95` and `45` instead.
- Before finishing, run concrete validation commands and confirm they passed.
- Do NOT commit or push changes in this step.

User prompt:
{{trigger.prompt}}

Definition of done:
- src/routes/+page.svelte is no longer the default starter page
- the repository contains a working SvelteKit app
- package.json and source files are present
- src/routes/+page.svelte includes the required data-demo hooks
- validation commands were run successfully, including NODE_ENV=development npm install, npm run check, and npm run build
- the final response states what was built and what commands you ran

agentConfig.name: claude-sandbox-prompt-build
agentConfig.instructions: You are working inside an existing git clone at /tmp/{{trigger.repo}}. Replace the default starter page immediately, build the requested app, verify it, rewrite files directly instead of patching them incrementally, include the required data-demo hooks in src/routes/+page.svelte for browser capture, and stop after the application files are ready for a deterministic git finalize step.

with.prompt:
Finalize the git repository in /tmp/{{trigger.repo}}.

Use ONE Bash tool call only. Copy this script exactly and do not rename paths or commands:

set -euo pipefail
cd /tmp/{{trigger.repo}}
git status --short
[ -f package.json ]
[ -f src/routes/+page.svelte ]
if rg -n 'Welcome to SvelteKit' src/routes/+page.svelte; then
  echo 'Starter page text is still present'
  exit 1
fi
rg -n 'data-demo="app-shell"|data-demo=\"app-shell\"' src/routes/+page.svelte
rg -n 'data-demo="primary-action"|data-demo=\"primary-action\"' src/routes/+page.svelte
rg -n 'data-demo="demo-state"|data-demo=\"demo-state\"' src/routes/+page.svelte
node <<'NODE'
const fs = require('fs');
const pkg = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json', 'utf8'));
pkg.private = true;
pkg.type = 'module';
pkg.scripts = {
  ...(pkg.scripts || {}),
  dev: 'vite dev',
  build: 'vite build',
  preview: 'vite preview',
  check: 'svelte-kit sync && svelte-check --fail-on-warnings'
};
pkg.devDependencies = {
  ...(pkg.devDependencies || {}),
  '@sveltejs/adapter-auto': '^3.3.1',
  '@sveltejs/kit': '^2.57.0',
  '@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte': '^5.1.1',
  'svelte': '^5.55.2',
  'vite': '^6.4.2',
  'svelte-check': '^4.3.1'
};
fs.writeFileSync('package.json', JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2) + '\n');
if (!fs.existsSync('jsconfig.json') && !fs.existsSync('tsconfig.json')) {
  fs.writeFileSync('jsconfig.json', JSON.stringify({
    extends: './.svelte-kit/tsconfig.json',
    compilerOptions: {
      allowJs: true,
      checkJs: true,
      esModuleInterop: true,
      forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true,
      moduleResolution: 'bundler',
      resolveJsonModule: true,
      skipLibCheck: true,
      sourceMap: true,
      strict: true
    }
  }, null, 2) + '\n');
}
NODE
rm -rf node_modules .svelte-kit
NODE_ENV=development npm install --no-audit --no-fund --loglevel=warn
npm run check
npm run build
git add -A
git diff --cached --stat
if git diff --cached --quiet; then
  echo 'NO_CHANGES_TO_COMMIT'
  exit 1
fi
git commit -m 'feat: implement prompt-driven sveltekit app'
git push origin main
git rev-parse HEAD
git ls-remote --heads origin main
echo 'FINALIZE COMPLETE'

Do not add any extra tool calls. If the Bash command fails, do not try recovery steps. Stop and report the failure. Report the commit hash only after the Bash command succeeds.

agentConfig.name: claude-finalize-push
agentConfig.instructions: You are performing a deterministic git finalize step. Use exactly one Bash tool call, do not edit files, and do not improvise.
